title: Bigram drill: th, in, er, an, re
description: Most frequent English letter pairs — train them as single motions
text: th th th in in in er er er an an an re re re the then this that with which when where there their other under under after father mother

tips.text: These five pairs make up roughly a fifth of every word pairing in English. Worth getting right.
Type each pair as a single move, not two separate keys. Smooth the joins and the clutter falls away.

tips.target: th th th in in in er er er an an an re re re

tips.text: If one pair fights you, pull it out and run it a dozen times alone. Fix the snag before it becomes a habit.

tips.target: the then this that with which when where there their other under under after father mother

character_tips.anna: Pairs are the bricks of speed. Make each one feel like a single keystroke. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: A practiced typist stops thinking in letters and starts thinking in pairs. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Two keys, one move. Quiet and clean, no theatrics. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Two letters stepping together, never apart. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
